hard.owner_title: Vice President, Asia Pacific Japan and ME
hard.next_step: 25% Confidence 26th Jun '26. Out reach from Napolean Manaog- looking to kick start an eval soon. ck to set up online discussion and aim to meet F2F when next in Manila
hard.ais_why: First Gen has made AI part of its enterprise technology strategy: a formal Digitalization Roadmap listing Data and AI as a strategic pillar, internal GenAI programs already rolling out, and investment in hyperautomation, RPA, enterprise data pipelines, advanced analytics, SAP roadmapping and AI governance, growing year over year.
hard.competitor: Do Nothing / Status Quo;Unnamed alternative provider;Coupa;GEP Worldwide;SAP Ariba

north_star_verdict.headline: First Gen re-engaged on a two-way, positive intro call on 1 July 2026 (FPH IT plus Napoleon Manaog), but this year the buyer is only building a Source-to-Pay roadmap with no approved budget, implementation is 'next year perhaps', and one other provider is also being consulted. The relationship is warm again, yet the 12 December 2026 close date is not credible: the buyer explicitly tied any purchase to next-year budget approval.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 14 April 2023, re-qualified from Initial Interest to Qualified on 26 June 2026; no validation-stage submission dates populated. Close date 12 December 2026 is 159 days out. On the 1 July 2026 call the buyer said implementation would be 'next year perhaps, if we get the budget approved' and that this year's goal is only a roadmap. A realistic path (roadmap and thought-leadership, agentic-AI demo, budget approval, RFP issue and response, shortlist and use-case validation, commercials, legal/InfoSec, contracting) cannot complete inside 159 days with no funded project. The deal is genuinely re-engaged and healthy for its early stage, so the sole real problem is that the date slips into 2027.
evidence: Avoma intro call 1 July 2026: FPH IT (Grace, business relationship management) and Napoleon Manaog (IT solutions) re-opened the conversation; two-way, positive, exploratory
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'Will it be implemented this year? No.' and 'is it gonna be next year? Perhaps, if we get the budget approved for it'
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'we're also talking to another provider ... we are still in the process of getting industry guidance'
Buyer on 1 July 2026 call: 'Patrick is no longer part of the company' and 'a relatively young group who's really relying on IT'
Field history 26 June 2026: StageName Initial Interest to Qualified and Amount 500000 to 350000 (by Carl Kimball)
AIS 8.8 Strong corroborated on the call: 'We're trying to have it in all of the enterprise solutions that we have'
Contact Role Renato Castillo (VP and Chief Risk Officer, Executive Sponsor): no documented engagement in the current cycle; budget authority not exercised

metrics.narrative: The business problem is confirmed but never quantified. On the 1 July 2026 call the FPH IT relationship manager (Grace) described procurement as 'mostly doing things manual' across the diverse Lopez Group conglomerate, whose 'primary challenge' is aligning processes across business units onto 'the same route'. Crucially the buyer said implementing a system to automate 'doesn't simply justify the cost of implementing that system for them', so they deployed smaller interim automation tools instead. No cost, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate or savings figure exists in any call or Salesforce field, and prior Next Steps (9 May 2024) show Finance and IT wanting an internal ROI discussion that was never quantified. Building a numeric S2P business case tied to their digitalization roadmap is the move that converts roadmap into a funded project.

economic_buyer.narrative: Renato Castillo (VP and Chief Risk Officer) is the Salesforce Executive Sponsor and the most credible budget-authority candidate, but he was absent from the 1 July 2026 re-opening and his sponsorship is undocumented in the current cycle. The call made clear that budget control now sits above the room: 'we're gonna have to have all of the approvals needed, the budget at that' and any purchase is 'next year perhaps, if we get the budget approved' (Grace, FPH IT, 1 July 2026). Procurement has fully turned over and is 'really relying on IT', so the funding decision runs through FPH IT and executive management, not the procurement holdovers. Re-engaging Renato Castillo and confirming who signs off the roadmap-to-project budget is the priority economic-buyer move.

decision_criteria.narrative: No structured evaluation criteria have been set this cycle because the buyer is at the roadmap-guidance stage, not vendor selection. What they DID articulate on 1 July 2026 is that they want 'industry guidance' and 'thought leadership' on 'what a good procurement end to end process should be', a view on 'how Zycus actually fits into a landscape', and specifically how the product handles a conglomerate's cross-entity process alignment and embedded agentic AI ('how does Zycus implement that pertaining to your solution'). These are the informal criteria to anchor to. Formal criteria will crystallise only when the RFP is funded, so shaping the roadmap now is how Zycus authors the eventual criteria in its favour.

decision_process.narrative: The process is early and IT-led: FPH IT (parent company, First Philippine Holdings) has inserted itself as the orchestrator, with Grace as the business-to-IT relationship link and Napoleon Manaog on the IT solutions side, both fronting for a new, young procurement team ('procurement is talking to right now'). The buyer described a staged path: this year produce a Source-to-Pay roadmap (one of the major enterprise-architecture work streams alongside lead-to-cash), then seek budget approval, then select a partner 'next year perhaps'. Approval depends on securing budget and management buy-in ('all of the approvals needed'). Reading the call sequence, the power has shifted from procurement (post-Patrick vacuum) to FPH IT as the gatekeeper of the roadmap and the vendor short-listing.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist yet: no MSA, Order Form, SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec review or legal step has been discussed, which is correct for a Qualified, roadmap-stage deal with no funded project. Current_Contract_Expiration is not populated, so there is no external forcing function. The only paper in play is informal: the buyer offered to send their existing S2P process documentation (originally built by the departed Patrick) for Zycus feedback. Visibility into the paper process will only open once budget is approved and an RFP is issued; until then there is nothing to redline.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is real but the buyer actively downplays its urgency. On 1 July 2026 the FPH IT relationship manager described largely manual procurement and, as a conglomerate, difficulty aligning 'diverse areas' onto a common route, calling process alignment 'one of their primary challenges'. However she also said automation alone 'doesn't simply justify the cost', and they have applied interim smaller automation tools to relieve pressure, which blunts urgency. The pain owner is the new procurement team (Caroline De Lunas and successor Soleil Yap) sponsored through FPH IT. Confirmed qualitative pain, no quantified impact, and a low stated compelling event, so surfacing a genuine trigger is the work.

champion.narrative: There is no active champion, and the prior one is gone. Patrick Dela Rama (Procurement Manager, Decision Maker, the developing champion of 2024) 'is no longer part of the company', confirmed on the 1 July 2026 call. Soleil Yap is named as the person who 'replaced Patrick' but is unproven and has shown no advocacy behaviour, and Caroline De Lunas remains in procurement but is not documented driving the deal. Napoleon Manaog (IT Officer) initiated the re-engagement and is the warmest current contact, but he sits in IT, not procurement, and his willingness to advocate specifically for Zycus is untested. Securing a procurement or FPH IT champion is the highest-order relationship move before any commercial step.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Patrick Dela Rama; Soleil M Yap; Caroline E. De Lunas

competition.narrative: The dominant current competitor is Do Nothing / status quo: the buyer has no approved budget, deployed interim automation tools, and framed this year as roadmap-only ('doesn't simply justify the cost'). A second, unnamed provider is also being consulted for 'industry guidance' (1 July 2026), a live parallel evaluation at the same roadmap stage. Salesforce lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide and SAP Ariba; Coupa historically had an internal advocate in Vince Mabutas (coached by Coupa, August 2023), but the procurement team has since fully turned over, lowering that advocacy's current weight. Our wedge is agentic AI: the buyer is explicitly agentic-AI hungry ('that's kind of where we wanna go to as an enterprise') and Zycus already embeds it (Anna, the autonomous negotiating agent; Merlin), backed by strong Philippine conglomerate references (BDO, Watsons and others) to counter status quo and the unnamed alternative.

deal_movement.summary: After a multi-year stall, First Gen re-engaged: Napoleon Manaog reached out on 26 June 2026 and a two-way intro call ran 1 July 2026. In the same window the deal was re-qualified (Initial Interest to Qualified) and the amount was cut from 500,000 to 350,000. Procurement has turned over (Patrick departed, Soleil Yap in), and FPH IT is now the entry point.

items.change: Napoleon Manaog (IT) reached out to kick-start an evaluation; Carl scheduled the intro discussion
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified (by Carl Kimball)
Two-way intro call held with FPH IT (Grace) and Napoleon Manaog; buyer set expectations to roadmap-only this year, purchase next year subject to budget
Buyer confirmed Patrick Dela Rama has left; Soleil Yap named as his replacement; Caroline De Lunas remains

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is Do Nothing / status quo: on 1 July 2026 the buyer said automation 'doesn't simply justify the cost', has no approved budget, and set implementation to 'next year perhaps'. A second unnamed provider is being consulted in parallel for roadmap guidance (medium, active). Coupa, GEP Worldwide and SAP Ariba remain on the Salesforce field; Coupa's historic internal advocate Vince Mabutas (coached August 2023) carries less current weight now that procurement has fully turned over. Certinal was a peripheral 2023 e-signature mention and has faded. No competitor is buyer-preferred today.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's stated expectations for this stage are thought leadership on an end-to-end S2P process, a view on how Zycus fits a conglomerate landscape, and an agentic-AI product demo. Zycus is well aligned on the agentic-AI expectation (embedded Anna/Merlin) and on references, but exposed on the buyer's cost-justification concern and their reluctance to reengineer processes.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage re-open with the champion gone and no funded project. The three highest-impact levers are rebuilding a procurement/FPH IT champion, converting the roadmap into a funded project with a quantified business case, and leading with agentic-AI differentiation.
flags: Rebuild the champion now: Patrick has left, so secure Soleil Yap (his named successor) and re-activate Caroline De Lunas before investing in a demo.
Convert roadmap to a funded project: co-build a quantified S2P business case tied to their digitalization roadmap and identify who approves the next-year budget.
Lead with agentic AI (Anna and Merlin) and Philippine conglomerate references (BDO, Watsons) to beat the status quo and the unnamed alternative provider.

champion_strength.summary: No proven champion. Patrick Dela Rama, the developing champion in 2024, has left (confirmed 1 July 2026). Soleil Yap reportedly replaced him but has shown no advocacy, and Caroline De Lunas remains but is passive. Napoleon Manaog (IT) is the warmest contact and drove the re-open, yet he is in IT, not procurement, and his advocacy is untested. Rebuilding a procurement or FPH IT champion is the priority.
champion_strength.champion: Napoleon Manaog (IT, warm but untested; procurement champion vacant after Patrick Dela Rama departed)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is well positioned on AI for this buyer: they are explicitly agentic-AI hungry and Carl seeded Anna (the autonomous negotiating agent for tail-spend, 3 to 4% savings) and embedded AI on the 1 July 2026 call, which the buyer welcomed as 'a good track'. The next step is to convert that interest into a concrete agentic-AI demo carried by the deal team.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.8 Strong is corroborated by the call: First Gen has a formal digitalization roadmap with Data and AI as a pillar, and the buyer said 'We're trying to have it in all of the enterprise solutions that we have' and that agentic AI is 'kind of where we wanna go to as an enterprise'. The field is if anything under-rated versus the appetite on the call.

items.detail: No approved budget; purchase explicitly tied to next-year funding, making the 12 December 2026 close date not credible.
Do-nothing / status-quo risk: buyer says automation 'doesn't justify the cost', has deployed interim tools, and does not want to fully reengineer processes.
Procurement team fully turned over: the developing champion Patrick Dela Rama left, and power has shifted to FPH IT with an unproven procurement successor (Soleil Yap).
Executive Sponsor Renato Castillo (VP and Chief Risk Officer) is not engaged in the current cycle, so no exercised budget authority.
An unnamed alternative provider is being consulted in parallel for the same roadmap guidance.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is genuinely live again after years of silence, but thin: one intro call read, IT-led, roadmap-stage, with budget and champion gaps. Warm two-way tone (Napoleon pulled Zycus back in) but no concrete buyer-owned dates.

Champion vacuum after Patrick's departure; procurement is new and relying on IT

critical_signals.text: First Gen re-opened after a multi-year silence on the 1 July 2026 intro call, now fronted by FPH IT (Grace) and Napoleon Manaog for a new, young procurement team.
The main threat is inertia: the buyer says automating procurement does not justify the cost and is running interim tools, while also taking roadmap guidance from one unnamed alternative provider.
Napoleon Manaog (IT) is the engaged entry point and champion Patrick Dela Rama has left, with Soleil Yap named as an unproven successor we have not yet met.
There is no approved budget, implementation is 'next year perhaps', and the deal value was cut from 500,000 to 350,000 on 26 June 2026.

scope_change.detail: Amount cut from 500,000 to 350,000 on 26 June 2026 (Carl Kimball) as the deal was re-qualified and the buyer signalled a phased, roadmap-first, budget-constrained approach rather than a big-bang S2P purchase this year.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A warm early re-open with real AI appetite but no funded project: First Gen is only building an S2P roadmap this year, the procurement champion has left, and any purchase waits on next-year budget, so the December close date is fiction.

win_position.text: This is an early, IT-led re-open where the buyer is buying roadmap guidance, not a platform: 'is it gonna be next year? Perhaps, if we get the budget approved' (Grace, FPH IT, Avoma 1 Jul 2026).
Strong AI fit is the wedge: AIS 8.8 and the buyer saying agentic AI is 'where we wanna go to as an enterprise', which Zycus already embeds via Anna and Merlin (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).

No champion and no engaged economic buyer: the developing champion Patrick Dela Rama has left and Executive Sponsor Renato Castillo is absent from the current cycle (Avoma 1 Jul 2026 + contact roles).
The buyer openly discounts the value case, saying automation 'doesn't simply justify the cost', and is also consulting one unnamed alternative provider (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Napoleon Manaog re-initiated contact on 26 June 2026 and joined a positive two-way intro call on 1 July 2026 after years of silence.
Engagement is still a generic roadmap/thought-leadership conversation with no agreed demo date and no buyer-named decision timeline.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer committed only to send their process documentation and a demo wish-list, and tied any purchase to next-year budget approval (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).
The deal value was reduced from 500,000 to 350,000 on 26 June 2026, a defensive, cost-conscious signal.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing is the primary risk: interim automation tools plus 'doesn't justify the cost' point to a no-decision outcome, not a loss to a rival.
Procurement turnover left a champion vacuum (Patrick gone, Soleil Yap unproven) and shifted control to FPH IT.

customer_commitment.evidence: Amount cut to 350,000 and purchase deferred to next-year budget; only low-cost commitments made (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).

deal_risk.evidence: No budget, no champion, status-quo bias, and a parallel unnamed provider (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: 'we're also talking to another provider ... we are still in the process of getting industry guidance' and no Zycus-specific lean; buyer is at neutral roadmap-guidance stage (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (manual procurement, conglomerate cross-entity alignment as a 'primary challenge'); no cost, savings, cycle-time or ROI figure, and the buyer says automation 'doesn't simply justify the cost' (Avoma 1 Jul 2026).
